message:
feat(parser): add support for advanced Python syntax

Add parser support for Flask real-world imports:
- Generator expressions in function calls: any(x for x in items)
- Parenthesized generator expressions: next((x for x in items), default)
- Tuple unpacking in comprehensions: for i, w in enumerate(items)
- Starred expressions in tuples/lists: (None, *reversed(items))
- Starred assignment targets: *parts, tail = items
- Multiple imports on one line: import io, os, sys
- Yield from syntax: yield from iterator
- Nonlocal keyword: nonlocal var_name
- Dotted exception types in tuples: except (OSError, mod.Error):

Also adds parseComprehensionTarget helper for tuple unpacking in
list comprehensions, dict comprehensions, and generator expressions.

files.patch:
@@ -33,6 +33,7 @@ pub const TokenType = enum {
     Raise,
     Lambda,
     Global,
+    Nonlocal,
     With,
     Is,
     Del,
@@ -356,6 +357,7 @@ pub const Lexer = struct {
             .{ "raise", .Raise },
             .{ "lambda", .Lambda },
             .{ "global", .Global },
+            .{ "nonlocal", .Nonlocal },
             .{ "with", .With },
             .{ "is", .Is },
             .{ "del", .Del },

@@ -222,6 +222,7 @@ pub const Parser = struct {
                 .Break => return try statements.parseBreak(self),
                 .Continue => return try statements.parseContinue(self),
                 .Global => return try statements.parseGlobal(self),
+                .Nonlocal => return try statements.parseNonlocal(self),
                 .With => return try statements.parseWith(self),
                 .Del => return try statements.parseDel(self),
                 .Ellipsis => return try statements.parseEllipsis(self),
